stem: A ruler measures to the nearest millimetre. A pencil is recorded as $47 \text{ mm}$ long. What is the absolute error of this measurement, in millimetres?
hint: Absolute error $= \dfrac{1}{2} \times$ precision of the instrument.
explanation: The ruler reads to the nearest $1 \text{ mm}$, so its precision is $1 \text{ mm}$. Absolute error $= \dfrac{1}{2} \times 1 = 0.5 \text{ mm}$.

stem: A kitchen scale measures to the nearest $10 \text{ g}$. A bag of rice shows a reading of $380 \text{ g}$. What is the absolute error of this measurement, in grams?
hint: Absolute error $= \dfrac{1}{2} \times$ the smallest unit the instrument can read.
explanation: The precision is $10 \text{ g}$. Absolute error $= \dfrac{1}{2} \times 10 = 5 \text{ g}$.

stem: A clinical thermometer measures to the nearest $0.5^\circ\text{C}$. A patient's temperature is recorded as $37.5^\circ\text{C}$. What is the absolute error of this reading, in degrees Celsius?
hint: Absolute error $= \dfrac{1}{2} \times$ the smallest readable unit.

stem: A stopwatch measures to the nearest $0.01 \text{ s}$. A sprinter's time is recorded as $11.23 \text{ s}$. What is the absolute error of this measurement, in seconds?
hint: Absolute error $= \dfrac{1}{2} \times$ the precision of the stopwatch.
explanation: The precision is $0.01 \text{ s}$. Absolute error $= \dfrac{1}{2} \times 0.01 = 0.005 \text{ s}$.

stem: A scale is accurate to the nearest $2 \text{ g}$ and records a mass of $40 \text{ g}$. Calculate the percentage error.
hint: First find the absolute error, then use $\% \text{ error} = \dfrac{\text{absolute error}}{\text{measurement}} \times 100\%$.

stem: A ruler is accurate to the nearest centimetre and records a length of $25 \text{ cm}$. Calculate the percentage error.
A thermometer is accurate to the nearest $2^\circ\text{C}$ and records a temperature of $20^\circ\text{C}$. Calculate the percentage error.
A stopwatch accurate to the nearest $0.1 \text{ s}$ records a time of $4.5 \text{ s}$. Calculate the percentage error. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.

A tape measure accurate to the nearest centimetre records a board as $60 \text{ cm}$ long. A steel rule accurate to the nearest millimetre records the same board as $598 \text{ mm}$. Calculate the percentage error for the tape measure. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.
hint: Absolute error of the tape measure $= \dfrac{1}{2} \times 1 \text{ cm} = 0.5 \text{ cm}$. Use the tape measure's recorded value in the denominator.
explanation: Absolute error $= 0.5 \text{ cm}$. Percentage error $= \dfrac{0.5}{60} \times 100 = 0.8\overline{3}\% \approx 0.83\%$. The steel rule has an absolute error of $0.5 \text{ mm} = 0.05 \text{ cm}$, giving a percentage error of $\dfrac{0.05}{59.8} \times 100 \approx 0.08\%$, so it is the more precise instrument.

stem: A digital scale accurate to the nearest $0.5 \text{ g}$ records a mass of $125 \text{ g}$. A laboratory balance accurate to the nearest $0.001 \text{ g}$ records the same mass as $124.936 \text{ g}$. Calculate the percentage error for the digital scale.
hint: Absolute error of the digital scale $= \dfrac{1}{2} \times 0.5 \text{ g} = 0.25 \text{ g}$.
explanation: Absolute error $= 0.25 \text{ g}$. Percentage error $= \dfrac{0.25}{125} \times 100 = 0.2\%$. The laboratory balance has an absolute error of $0.0005 \text{ g}$, giving a percentage error of approximately $0.0004\%$. The balance is far more precise.

stem: Two thermometers measure the same room temperature. Thermometer A (accurate to the nearest $1^\circ\text{C}$) records $24^\circ\text{C}$. Thermometer B (accurate to the nearest $0.1^\circ\text{C}$) records $24.0^\circ\text{C}$. Which thermometer is more precise?
options: Thermometer A, because it uses whole numbers
Thermometer B, because it has a smaller absolute error and a smaller percentage error
Both are equally precise, because they record the same temperature
Thermometer A, because a larger absolute error indicates a wider range of possible values
answer: Thermometer B, because it has a smaller absolute error and a smaller percentage error
hint: Calculate the percentage error for each: $\% \text{ error} = \dfrac{\text{absolute error}}{\text{measurement}} \times 100\%$.
explanation: Thermometer A: absolute error $= 0.5^\circ\text{C}$, percentage error $= \dfrac{0.5}{24} \times 100 \approx 2.08\%$. Thermometer B: absolute error $= 0.05^\circ\text{C}$, percentage error $= \dfrac{0.05}{24} \times 100 \approx 0.21\%$. Thermometer B has the smaller percentage error and is therefore more precise.

stem: A steel rod is measured by two instruments. Instrument P (accurate to the nearest $1 \text{ mm}$) records $500 \text{ mm}$. Instrument Q (accurate to the nearest $10 \text{ mm}$) records $500 \text{ mm}$. Which statement correctly describes the comparison?
options: Instrument Q is more precise because a larger unit of measurement reduces human error
Both instruments have the same percentage error because they recorded the same value
Instrument P is more precise because it has a smaller percentage error
Instrument Q is more precise because it rounds to a larger place value
answer: Instrument P is more precise because it has a smaller percentage error
hint: Calculate the percentage error for each instrument: $\% \text{ error} = \dfrac{\text{absolute error}}{\text{measurement}} \times 100\%$.
explanation: Instrument P: absolute error $= 0.5 \text{ mm}$, percentage error $= \dfrac{0.5}{500} \times 100 = 0.1\%$. Instrument Q: absolute error $= 5 \text{ mm}$, percentage error $= \dfrac{5}{500} \times 100 = 1\%$. Instrument P has the smaller percentage error ($0.1\% < 1\%$) and is therefore more precise.
